$comment: Shared golden vectors for cycle prediction. Single source of truth consumed by BOTH the TypeScript reference test (src/services/cycle-prediction-reference.test.ts) and, by design, ovumcy-web's Go reference test (internal/services/cycles_reference_test.go). The two prediction implementations — cycle-prediction-policy.ts and cycles.go — are hand-parallel ports; these vectors enforce parity so a change to either that diverges from the other fails CI. Any prediction-math change MUST update this file, docs/cycle-prediction.md, and both reference tests in the same change. Dates are calendar dates (YYYY-MM-DD), timezone-independent. See docs/cycle-prediction.md for the derivation of each case.

vectors.name: 15-day cycle, luteal 14 clamped to 10, window clamped to period start (non-exact)
14-day cycle -> below the 15-day floor, no prediction

projection.$comment: ADDITIVE section (lands in ovumcy-app via its twin PR). Pins the projection/anchor layer that production layers ON TOP of the base window math in `vectors`. Given a cycle-length history plus `lastPeriodStart`, `today`, and an IANA `timezone`, it locks: (1) the median-first prediction length (predictedCycleLength in cycles.go / cycle-prediction-policy.ts), (2) the projected current-cycle start and 1-based cycle day (ProjectCycleStart), (3) the displayed next-period date derived from the UN-shifted projected start, and (4) the ovulation date after the single forward roll (ShiftCycleStartToFutureOvulation) that keeps ovulation on/after `today`. All arithmetic is calendar-day based and DST-immune, so the Europe/Berlin 2026-03-29 spring-forward case must project identically to a UTC run. Histories stay within the recent-cycle window (<= 6) so no tail truncation applies. Consumed by ovumcy-web's TestCycleProjection_GoldenVectors; ovumcy-app's existing reference test decodes only `vectors` and ignores this key until its twin consumer lands. See docs/cycle-prediction.md.

vectors.name: median-vs-mean [28,28,28,28,60]: projects from median 28, not mean 34
DST spring-forward (Europe/Berlin 2026-03-29): boundary rollover stays calendar-correct
boundary-day projection: today exactly one cycle out rolls to cycle day 1
